pattern: Flattening the matrix in row-major order and re-chunking by the new width gives the reshape, valid only when the element counts match.
hints: The reshape is legal only if r*c equals the total number of elements in the original matrix.
Read all elements in row-major order into a single flat list, then refill a new r-by-c grid in the same order.
If r*c != m*n, return the original matrix. Otherwise map each flat index k to row k//c and column k%c (or chunk the flat list into rows of length c).

edge_inputs.description: reshape a row vector into a column vector
same total but matching identity reshape
r*c larger than element count is illegal

stress.description: random matrix with a target shape that is sometimes legal and sometimes not
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randrange(1, max(2, min(size, 20)))
    n = rng.randrange(1, max(2, min(size, 20)))
    mat = [[rng.randrange(-1000, 1001) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    total = m * n
    if rng.random() < 0.6:
        # legal: pick divisor pair of total
        divs = [d for d in range(1, total + 1) if total % d == 0]
        r = rng.choice(divs)
        c = total // r
    else:
        r = rng.randrange(1, 300)
        c = rng.randrange(1, 300)
    return [mat, r, c]
stress.note: mostly legal reshapes plus some illegal ones
